# 2026-02-20

## LinkedIn Setup
- Logged into Dwayne's LinkedIn (dwayne@urbanbutter.com) via openclaw browser
- Password was reset due to security check — new password sent via DM
- Old password was accidentally shared in group chat — deleted
- Profile still shows "President and CEO at Urbanbutter Creative Group inc." — Dwayne updating
- Published first SolveWorks post (origin story + what SolveWorks does + link)
- Plan: 3-4 posts/week going forward

## SolveWorks Leads
- Mike from Rylem (staffing agency) — strong lead, asking implementation questions
- Wants auto-extraction of leads matching criteria + automated outreach sequence
- Dwayne sent response about ICP-based auto-prospecting
- Leads coming from Darryl's YPO network — NOT just small business anymore
- Updated LinkedIn copy to be size-agnostic (removed "small business" framing)

## SolveWorks X Account — LIVE
- Handle: @Solveworks_io
- Email: dwayne@solveworks.io (Google sign-in)
- Password reset and set — sent via DM
- Bio set, display name changed to "SolveWorks", website linked
- Still needs: profile pic (SolveWorks logo), banner image
- Posting autonomously — brand voice, AI/business content

## Auto-checkpoint (6:57 AM)
- Committed: SolveWorks favicons + logo files (14 files)
- Pushed to origin, backed up private files

## SolveWorks Lead Gen Work (7-8 AM)
- Created YPO one-pager content (ypo-one-pager.md)
- Designed premium dark-navy HTML/PDF version (ypo-one-pager-designed.html/pdf)
- Wrote 4 LinkedIn posts for the week (linkedin-posts-2026-02-20.md)
- Revenue idea generated: YPO Referral Flywheel (research/revenue-ideas/2026-02-20.md)
- Dwayne's positioning: local-first, custom-built, managed service — NOT generic cloud AI
- Gateway issues blocked sub-agent spawning (pairing required errors)

## Auto-checkpoint (8:57 AM)
- Committed: YPO one-pager, LinkedIn posts, 7 Cellars private event research, SolveWorks data
- Pushed to origin, backed up private files

## Mission Control Dashboard
- **Concept:** From YouTube video about OpenClaw Mission Control (Aj6hoC9JaLI)
- **Decision:** Build as static pages on solveworks.io (GitHub Pages), NOT local hosting or Vercel
- **URL pattern:** solveworks.io/mission/ (Mika), solveworks.io/7cellars/ (partners), solveworks.io/clientname/ (future clients)
- **Architecture:** Pure HTML/CSS/JS, password-protected, JSON data synced every 5 min from Mac Mini
- **7 Cellars dashboard:** Next build after Mission Control — includes kanban (replaces Trello), comments/notes for partners, inventory, sales, meeting summaries
- **SolveWorks product:** Every client gets their own dashboard — key retention play and deliverable
- **Sub-agent building it now:** mission-control-static

## Auto-checkpoint (10:57 AM)
- Committed: HeyGen avatar video, 7 Cellars dashboard, Mission Control dashboard (17 files)
- Pushed to origin, backed up private files

# 2026-02-19 (Wednesday)

## Auto-checkpoints
- 04:57 — Committed 2 new research files (revenue-ideas, website-audits) and pushed. Private backup ran.
- 05:57 — Created today's memory file. No uncommitted changes.

## McAdoo's Lead — Tyler
- Email: tylert@mcadoos.ca
- AI Audit score: 85/100, 21 hrs/week savings potential
- Key pain points: manual customer comms, 10+ hrs/week admin, no data tracking, team resistance to new tools
- Proposal being generated (no pricing yet — Brody doing the call)
- **IMPORTANT for final proposal:** Include AI-powered proposal generation as a solution — Tyler's team already uses templates for proposals, automate that. Dwayne flagged this specifically.

## LESSON: Proposal Projections Must Be Realistic
- Revaly proposal had savings projections HIGHER than their actual expenses — killed credibility
- Darryl's feedback: "lost credibility then built it" because of inaccurate numbers
- **Rules going forward:**
  1. NEVER fabricate or estimate savings without real data from a discovery call
  2. If no discovery call data, use conservative ranges or say "to be determined after discovery"
  3. Better to underestimate than overestimate — credibility > impressive numbers
  4. Always have a discovery call before final proposal
  5. Don't hallucinate revenue/expense/savings figures
- This applies to ALL proposals, not just Revaly

## Revaly Discovery Call — Next Week
- Brody doing discovery call with one of the Revaly owners
- Building a personal agent (like Drew's setup)
- Use the new discovery template for this call
- Remember: previous Revaly proposal had inflated numbers — this time we get REAL data from the call
- Darryl owns Revaly — every interaction is a SolveWorks demo

## SolveWorks Pricing — CONFIRMED
- **Setup:** $1,500 (one-time)
- **Monthly:** $250/month (maintenance — new skills, updates, support)
- Source: Dwayne's friend pitched to YPO group, multiple interested, this pricing suggested
- YPO members run businesses $5M+ revenue
- Multiple booking calls coming in from this

## SolveWorks Pricing — UPDATED
- **Setup:** $1,500 (one-time)
- **Option A:** $250/month — client pays their own Anthropic API costs
- **Option B:** $450/month — fully managed, SolveWorks covers AI/API costs
- Both include: maintenance, new skills, updates, priority support

## Case Studies — Real Clients
- Replace old fictional case studies with real ones
- **Dwayne S.** — 3 businesses (urbanbutter, 7 Cellars, SolveWorks) with one agent
- **Anthony M.** (Drew) — Senior exec, personal agent deployment
- Wait a few days then ask Anthony for a quote
- Follow up: ~Feb 22-23 for Anthony's quote

## SolveWorks — Pricing in USD
- All pricing is USD standard
- Exceptions case by case at Dwayne's discretion

## SolveWorks Deployment Model — CONFIRMED
- Mac Mini fleet model: buy, build here, ship to client, plug and play
- $1,500 setup (covers Mini ~$600 + build time)
- $250/month — client pays own Anthropic API key
- $450/month — fully managed, SolveWorks covers API costs
- All pricing in USD
- Kill switch via Tailscale + SSH + OpenClaw config control
- Every Mini ships with: Tailscale, SSH keys, auto-start, SolveWorks-controlled config
- Client just plugs in ethernet + power
- Remote management via Tailscale for updates, skills, troubleshooting

## Proposals Sent — Feb 19
- **McAdoo's (Tyler)** — tylert@mcadoos.ca — construction/landscaping
- **Rylem (Mike)** — miked@rylem.com — Seattle staffing agency  
- **HitLights (Joe)** — jfreeman@hitlights.com — LED lighting products, 5K+ installers
- All pre-call proposals, framed as initial assessments
- All came from YPO network via Darryl's friend
- Discovery calls booked with Brody

# 2026-02-18 (Wednesday)

## Morning
- 4:57 AM: Auto-checkpoint ran, committed 2 research files (revenue-ideas, website-audits)

- 6:57 PM: Auto-checkpoint — committed 2 new SolveWorks proposals (Apollo Originals, CMS MacTech), private backup pushed

## Afternoon
- 4:32 PM: Auto-checkpoint — 33 new files committed (Cin7 import screenshots, California producer bottle photos, cin7-california-import.csv). Cin7 product import work happening.

## Afternoon

## Afternoon

  - Cantina Tramin Vivino ratings research
  - SolveWorks logo iterations (v1-v3) + brand generation tools
  - SW logo v2 in output/
- Private backup also ran (1 file updated)

## Afternoon

- 10:06 AM: TRT injection reminder sent

## Midday
- 12:57 PM: Auto-checkpoint — 9 files committed:
  - 7 Cellars inventory CSVs (California + main final)
  - Wine naming SOP (PDF + changelog + page images)
  - CMS MacTech research brief
  - SolveWorks Shopify retailer proposal
- Private backup also pushed
- 10:06 AM: TRT injection reminder sent
- 10:57 AM: Auto-checkpoint — committed new SolveWorks blog post (what-is-an-ai-agent) + blog index update. Private backup ran.

## Mid-Morning
- 8:57 AM: Auto-checkpoint. Committed SolveWorks pricing tiers update + 2 new proposals (Dr. Sutherland v3, Greener Gardens v2). Private backup also pushed.
- 6:57 AM: Auto-checkpoint — 13 new files committed (product enrichment scripts, California wine data, 2 new SOPs: purchasing-guardrails + wine-intake-process). Private backup ran.

## First Inbound SolveWorks Lead! 🔥 (~10:30 AM)
- Lead came in minutes after Dwayne posted the "Beyond Chatbots" blog to Facebook
- Lead is in real estate, brother has a welding company
- Booked a call via Calendly
- Info being shared with Brody
- Blog post → Facebook → lead in under an hour. Content marketing WORKS.

## Day 2 Progress (~6 AM - 11 AM)

### 7 Cellars
- Fixed "Tre STelle" typo on Shopify ✅
- Product descriptions sub-agent running — enriching all 210 products from 1-sentence to 3-5 paragraphs
- California wines: 20 wines researched, descriptions being written, Vivino scores pulled and sent to Dwayne
- Tallyn receiving photos from California supplier today — will upload to Shopify once received
- Wine intake SOP documented (7cellars/sops/wine-intake-process.md)
- Purchasing guardrails SOP documented (7cellars/sops/purchasing-guardrails.md)
- Blog schedule: Decanting this Friday, Cab Sauv deep dive next week

### SolveWorks
- Blog post "Beyond Chatbots" published to solveworks.io ✅
- Had wrong styling (white bg) — restyled to match dark theme. LESSON: always check existing templates first.
- Dwayne posted to Facebook → FIRST INBOUND LEAD within minutes! Real estate guy, brother has welding company. Booked a Calendly call.
- Info shared with Brody

### Infrastructure
- Created DECISIONS.md — all confirmed decisions in one place
- Created ERRORS.md — lessons learned, never repeat
- Updated AGENTS.md boot sequence to include both files
- Context switching between chats was losing recent work — fix: write to memory in real-time, every session reads DECISIONS.md + ERRORS.md + today's memory
- Anthropic API had a transient 500 error — resolved itself

### Overnight Jobs (confirmed working)
- All 3 ran successfully: OpenClaw Pulse, Website Audit, Revenue Ideas
- Morning briefing compiled and delivered to Dwayne's DM at 6 AM ✅

# 2026-02-17 — Monday

## Notes
- Created daily memory file at 5:57 AM heartbeat
- No recent file changes or uncommitted work

- Throughout day: SolveWorks site work happening — new blog post, industry pages (accounting, contractors, healthcare, property mgmt, real estate, shopify-retail), case studies, Drew Millington proposal

- New: solveworks-clients/drew-millington/, california wines research, inventory CSV, backup script, security audit
- Busy SolveWorks day — lots of site work (industry pages, proposals, blog)
- 6:57 PM: Push to origin FAILED — 7cellars/images.zip (101MB) exceeds GitHub 100MB limit. Needs removal from history or .gitignore + LFS. Backup script ran OK.
- Evening: More work done — Vivino scores for 7 Cellars wines, SolveWorks pricing research (md+pdf), SolveWorks docs (deployment security, managed agent architecture, pricing tiers)
- Skipping git push until images.zip resolved; private backup running fine
- Late evening: 20 California bottle photos organized (Predator, Round Hill, Rutherford Ranch, Scott Family, Four Virtues, Torcia), Drew Millington install guide, TOOLS.md updated
- Productive day — SolveWorks site buildout + 7 Cellars product/inventory work

## TODO — Later Today
- **Set up solveworks.io email** (Google Workspace)
  - dwayne@solveworks.io + brody@solveworks.io

## SolveWorks Website Overhaul

- Changed "20+ Years in Business" to "20+ Years Business Experience"

- "Text your store/agent" angle added as core messaging across all industry pages
- Proposal generator skill created at ~/clawd/skills/solveworks-proposal/

## Drew Millington — First OpenClaw Client (Free Test Build)
- Friend of Dwayne's, multiple businesses (NMBR payroll, setter/coaching, investing)
- Wants: sales call coaching (Sandler), NMBR lead intel, setter lead gen, TSX dividend research, daily Quebec French lessons, payroll marketing intel
- Proposal built: solveworks.io/proposals/drew-millington-proposal.html
- Discovery call script created for Brody: solveworks-collab/sales/drew-millington-discovery-script.md

- Drew has a dedicated Mac laptop for OpenClaw
- Remote access plan: Tailscale + Screen Sharing on implementation Zoom

## SolveWorks Pricing Discussion
- Mom and pop Shopify shops can't afford enterprise pricing
- Landed on ~$500-1000 setup + $150-250/mo for small retailers
- Volume play: template the setup, get 10-15 shops at $150-200/mo
- Key selling point: "Text your store like you'd text an employee"

## TODO — Flagged for Later Today
- Set up solveworks.io email via Google Workspace (Porkbun DNS)
- dwayne@solveworks.io + brody@solveworks.io

## Tailscale Installed ✅ (~8:30 AM)
- Installed on Mac Mini, iPhone, iPad Pro
- All 3 devices connected to private mesh network
- Mac Mini: 100.127.230.68
- iPhone: 100.125.32.10  
- iPad Pro: 100.99.186.49
- Account: dwayne@
- Major security upgrade — Mac Mini no longer needs open ports

## Morning Session (~7:30-9:30 AM) — Highly Productive

### Partners Chat Fix
- Deleted morning briefing from partners chat (contained Revaly/SolveWorks info — Darryl is Revaly owner)

### Shopify — Grape Varieties Populated ✅

### Google Sheets Created

- Big container (40'+20') arriving in weeks — this is the dry run

### Cayman Gateway Payment Provider

- Will set up Shopify payments once instructions received

### Google Workspace for SolveWorks

### Security Hardening

  - dwayne-ubcg/clawd-private (sensitive files — MEMORY.md, TOOLS.md, memory/)

- Disaster recovery doc being created on Google Drive

### SolveWorks Architecture — Major Strategy Session
- Managed agent model: sandboxed clients, license key heartbeat system
- Option 2 chosen: license check on heartbeat, agent self-disables if subscription invalid
- Full spec docs created: managed-agent-architecture.md, deployment-security-checklist.md, pricing-tiers.md
- Google Docs being created for Dwayne: architecture + pricing/projections
- Pricing tiers: Starter $450/mo, Pro $750/mo, Enterprise $1,500/mo
- $10K/mo goal achievable at ~15 Starter or mix of 8 Starter + 4 Pro + 1 Enterprise

# 2026-02-16

## Morning — Post-Update Restart

## Dwayne's Grok Video Assessment
- File: 352x640, ~6s, h264, 556KB
- Content: Two hands clinking white wine glasses against sunset with sailboat (Red Sail catamaran)
- Issues: Too low-res for hero banner (need 1080p+), AI artifacts on hands/glasses, portrait-only
- Verdict: Not usable as-is for Shopify hero. Could work for Instagram story/reel.

## Auto-Checkpoint 08:57

- TOOLS.md updated
- SolveWorks proposal generator updated

## Revaly Enterprise Security Blueprint (~2-5 PM)
- Created branded PDF responding to Darryl's security concerns about OpenClaw
- Used SolveWorks HTML proposal template (revaly-proposal.html as base)
- Content: 7 pages — exec summary, threat acknowledgment, 8 enterprise controls, 30-day pilot, CTA
- Files: `solveworks-site/proposals/revaly-security-blueprint.html` + `.pdf` (690KB)
- Pushed to GitHub: `dwayne-ubcg/solveworks` repo
- Researched OpenClaw 2026.2.12 release (40+ security patches) — Dwayne wanted this added as proof of active security development
- **Google Doc attempt rejected by Dwayne** — plain doc format unacceptable, must use branded HTML template
- Dwayne flagged process inefficiency: I tried Drive uploads/browser paste before checking memory for established Apps Script workflow
- Updated TOOLS.md with "Google Docs via Apps Script — STANDARD WORKFLOW" section
- Spawned runbook-builder sub-agent to create RUNBOOKS.md
- **BLOCKED: Cannot send local PDF via Telegram** — `message` tool rejects all local paths. Tried /tmp, ~/clawd/uploads/, workspace paths — all denied. Need `mediaLocalRoots` config or alternative delivery.
- **TODO**: Still need to update blueprint with OpenClaw 2026.2.12 security section, regenerate PDF, and find delivery method
- GitHub raw link fallback: https://github.com/dwayne-ubcg/solveworks/blob/main/proposals/revaly-security-blueprint.pdf

### Telegram mediaLocalRoots Discovery
- BlueBubbles docs mention `mediaLocalRoots` config for allowing local file sends
- Telegram channel likely needs similar config — check if `channels.telegram.mediaLocalRoots` exists in config schema

# 2026-02-15

## Morning Session

### "The Last Pour" Collection — CREATED ✅
- Smart collection ID: 649668493604, handle: "the-last-pour"
- Rules: inventory 1-3 units (greater than 0, less than 4)
- Auto-populates — 34 wines initially qualified (Bordeaux '95, Brunello '16, Nuit-Saint-Georges '11, etc.)
- Added to homepage after collection cards section
- Dwayne loved this idea from the morning briefing

### New Arrivals Collection — Fixed ✅
- Changed from manual tags to smart collection sorted by created-desc
- Now auto-shows newest products added to Shopify
- Homepage carousel pulls top 8 automatically

### OpenClaw Updated 2026.2.12 → 2026.2.14 ✅
- Key improvements: Telegram polls, memory security hardening, memory leak fixes, sub-agent reliability, media delivery fix, compaction safety timeout, cron fixes
- Solid maintenance/security release

### Mobile Hero Video — SWAPPED ✅

- Original photo: 13-redsail-sunset-cheers-whitewine.png → cropped to 9:16 (1008x1792)
- Video uploaded to Shopify: gid://shopify/Video/45081438159140

### Desktop Hero Video — Kept Current

- Upscaled to 1920x1080 but too soft for luxury brand
- Decision: keep wine swirl on desktop, sunset cheers on mobile
- Plan: shoot proper 4K video on next Cayman trip

# 2026-02-14

## Afternoon Session (continued)

### Cin7 ↔ Shopify Integration — COMPLETED ✅
- Created "Shopify Online" customer in Cin7 (Name: Shopify Online, Sale account: 4000: Sales, Tax rule: Tax Exempt, Currency: KYD, Payment term: 15 days, Sale price tier: Tier 1)
- Verified integration settings saved: Load orders ON, Customer: Shopify Online, Update stock levels ON, MASTER: Source system, Location: Default
- Integration is now fully configured and live

### Shopify Shipping Rates — COMPLETED ✅
- Added zone "Grand Cayman • Cayman Islands" to General profile (fulfills from "10 market street" location)
- Rate 1: Free Delivery — orders $100.00+ → Free
- Rate 2: Standard Delivery — orders $0.00–$99.99 → $10.00
- Profile saved and confirmed ("Profile updated" toast)
- NOTE: "7 Cellars" location (Breezy Castle) still shows "Not shipping from this location" — may need rates added there too if that's the active fulfillment location

### Google Business Profile — Still Unverified ⚠️
- Profile exists: 7 Cellars, Wine store, correct address/phone/website
- Status: "NOT PUBLICLY VISIBLE" — verification code 00777 didn't work
- Need Roberto or Kyle to re-verify on-ground (phone call to 345-526-8466 or postcard)
- Description still empty, hours not set — can add once verified or before

### Facebook/Instagram
- Dwayne set up 7 Cellars Facebook page, linked to Instagram
- All Instagram posts auto-cross-post to Facebook
- Planning paid ads on both platforms soon for private client acquisition

### Sub-agents Spawned
- **Reece cayman captions** (`agent:main:subagent:c2f1949f-7d74-44c2-a325-83dddd74ef6b`) — Writing Instagram captions for 18 edited Cayman lifestyle photos → `7cellars/instagram/cayman-lifestyle-captions.md`
- **Reece ad campaign** (`agent:main:subagent:2893c338-5752-4df2-a460-820f22e8d12b`) — Full FB/Insta ad campaign strategy for private client acquisition → `7cellars/marketing/ad-campaign-strategy.md`

### Tallyn Cin7 Wholesale Sales Guide — Confirmed Sent ✅
- Full step-by-step SOP delivered to Tallyn's Telegram group (-5036963456)
- Covers: New Sale → Customer → Location (Main Warehouse!) → Products → Authorize → Pick/Pack/Ship → Invoice → Payment

### Key Quote from Dwayne
- "Man we are crushing it" — morale high, momentum strong
- Wants killer ad campaign to bring in new private clients
- Confirmed tasks 1, 2, 4 from suggested list — Saturday (Valentine's Day)

## Summary
Quiet Saturday. Mostly heartbeat/checkpoint cycles.

## Activity
- Morning briefing sent via cron at 6 AM
- TRT injection reminder sent to alerts channel
- Auto-committed new files throughout the day:
  - `7cellars/shopify-gap-analysis.md` — Shopify gap analysis
  - `7cellars/sops/cin7-wholesale-sales-guide.md` — Cin7 wholesale guide
  - `7cellars/instagram/cayman-lifestyle-captions.md` — Instagram captions
  - `7cellars/marketing/ad-campaign-strategy.md` — Ad campaign strategy (758 lines)
- Binary files left untracked: xlsx, PDF, image directories

## Notes
- No direct messages from Dwayne today
- Monday Feb 16 is a holiday (Family Day / everyone out of office)

# 2026-02-13 (Thursday)

## Shopify Product Sync — MAJOR MILESTONE
- **Round 1:** Sub-agent synced 214/439 products before session expired (~10:05 AM AST)
- **Round 2:** Spawned second sub-agent (label: shopify-sync-round2, session: `agent:main:subagent:a16129b2-a99a-4d75-be0e-f92ea1a7c3ca`) at ~11:20 AM
- By 11:36 AM, **430 products live** on Shopify with proper KYD retail pricing
- **Deleted 11 old placeholder products** that had $0 prices (Dwayne flagged this)
- Sub-agent still finishing last ~9 products
- **Dwayne wanted it live for partner call at noon** — made the deadline

## Shopify OAuth Token Obtained (~9:44 AM)
- Got `shpat_` token via OAuth flow using mika-3 app version
- Deployed app with localhost:3456 redirect, ran Node.js callback server
- Saved to .env. API confirmed working: shop "7 Cellars", domain "7cellars.ky"
- Actual internal Shopify domain: `tbmxng-y6.myshopify.com` (not 7-cellars-2)
- **E-COMMERCE BLOCKER REMOVED**

## Key Decisions
- Retail only on Shopify for now (Dwayne confirmed)
- Publish all products even without payments set up (Dwayne confirmed)
- Remove old placeholder products (done — 11 deleted)
- NEVER put tokens/credentials in chat (Dwayne flagged hard after shpat_ leaked, had to delete ~8 messages)

## Partners Chat
- Kyle asked about Tallyn's title → "Director of Operations & Client Success"
- Active discussion about email introductions for Tallyn

## Cron Jobs
- shopify-sync-check scheduled for 11:30 AM AST (one-shot, job ID: 602e57b6-24d0-4ed5-92d2-80fa1370a105)
- Morning briefing fired at 6 AM as usual

## Shopify Publishing Fix (~11:52 AM)
- Dwayne reported "no products found" on site
- Root cause: all 214 products were in **draft** status, not active
- Round 2 sub-agent created duplicates with $0 prices AND in draft — double fail
- Deleted 216+14 = ~230 duplicate $0 products across two cleanup passes
- Published remaining 214 products: used bash curl loop (python scripts kept getting SIGKILL'd — memory/process limits)
- Final state: **214 active products, 0 drafts, 0 $0 prices**
- Image fix sub-agent running (label: shopify-images-fix, session: `agent:main:subagent:0c8c8fd5-2850-4d91-bf99-c0feef0e28b4`) — 166 products missing images

## Theme Template Fix (~1:48 PM)
- Product descriptions were in closed accordion, not visible by default
- Parker/Vivino score blocks used empty metafields (product.metafields.custom.parker_score)
- Fixed: changed description display_type to "standard", moved it up after price in block_order
- Updated via Shopify themes API: PUT themes/186059653412/assets.json

## vino.ky Images PERMANENTLY DEAD (~1:42 PM)
- Dwayne confirmed vino.ky was redirected to 7cellars.ky — old site is down
- 506 out of 681 image URLs were from vino.ky — all dead
- 64 products have images (already on Shopify CDN from round 1 sub-agent)
- 150 products still missing images
- Web scraping Wine-Searcher/wine.com FAILED — sites block automated scraping (0/80 found)
- Brave Image Search API working — 9/80 found and uploaded as base64 (~11% hit rate)
- Script running: `/tmp/brave_image_search.py` (session: grand-comet)
- **Dwayne wants all images self-hosted** — download and upload as base64 to Shopify CDN, no external URL dependencies ever

## Key Shopify Lessons Learned
- `status: "active"` ≠ published to storefront. Must also set `published_at` timestamp
- `published_at: null` means product exists but invisible on Online Store
- Need `read_publications` scope to use publications API (we don't have it)
- Setting `published_at` via product PUT works as workaround
- Brave Search image API: works but limited hit rate for obscure wines. Free tier: 2000 queries/month
- Round 2 sub-agent created DUPLICATES with $0 and draft status — catastrophic. Must verify sub-agent output.

## Process Execution Lessons
- Long-running Python scripts with requests get SIGKILL'd (likely memory limit or OOM)
- **Use bash curl loops instead** — lighter weight, doesn't accumulate memory
- Write IDs to temp file first, then loop with curl — more reliable than Python for bulk API ops
- Always check both `status=active` AND `status=draft` counts separately

## Lessons Learned
- `source ~/clawd/.env` doesn't work in Python heredocs via exec — need `export $(grep ... .env | xargs)` pattern
- python-dotenv not installed — use shell env export instead
- Sub-agent progress files may not reflect actual progress if sub-agent writes in batches — always verify via Shopify API count

## Pre-compaction Dump #2 (18:25 AST)

### Cin7 API Lessons (CRITICAL for future use)
- Stock adjustment endpoint: POST then PUT `/ExternalApi/v2/stockAdjustment`
- Each line MUST have `"Location": "Main Warehouse"` — fails silently without it
- Create as DRAFT, then PUT with same Lines + Status: COMPLETED
- No negative quantities — can't undo via API, must use UI
- Batch size 20 lines per request works reliably
- Rate limit: 1 req/sec is safe
- Vintage is NOT stored as a standard field (despite appearing in upload script output)
- Product names have "Producer - Product (Bottle/Case)" format
- Location ID for Main Warehouse: 63b3362b-915f-40fe-a7ff-5bb7a6c45ec3

### Shopify Inventory API
- `POST /inventory_levels/set.json` with location_id, inventory_item_id, available
- Location ID: 113126539556 (hardcoded — our token lacks read_locations scope)
- Get inventory_item_id from product variant
- Scopes we have: write_content, write_inventory, write_products, write_themes (NO read_publications, NO read_locations)

### Matching Strategy That Worked (push-stock-v2.py)
- Normalize names: strip accents, remove (Bottle/Case), lowercase, alphanum only
- Group Cin7 products by (normalized_short_name, size_type) 
- Match CSV rows to groups, pop from group in order (handles same-name-different-vintage)
- Word overlap >= 50% as fallback
- This got 210/210 matched with 0 unmatched
